content_en:
Al-Insaf fi Ma'rifat ar-Rajih min al-Khilaf ('The Equity in Knowing the Preponderant Position from the Scholarly Disagreements') is the most important work on internal Hanbali legal disputes and one of the most valuable reference works in Islamic jurisprudence for anyone seeking to understand how the Hanbali school resolved its internal scholarly differences. Its author, 'Ala' ad-Din Abu al-Hasan 'Ali ibn Sulayman al-Mardawi (817–885 AH / 1414–1480 CE), was one of the most learned Hanbali scholars of the ninth century AH and is considered the authoritative resolver (murajjih) of disputed points within the Hanbali school.

Al-Mardawi composed Al-Insaf as a commentary and completion of al-Muqni' by Ibn Qudamah, integrating into it all the views expressed in the major Hanbali works up to his time — particularly Al-Mughni (Ibn Qudamah), Al-Furu' (Ibn Muflih), and Al-Insiraf (Ibn Qundus) — and then judging which opinion is the most preponderant (rajih) based on the strength of evidence and the weight of scholarly consensus within the school.

The great value of Al-Insaf is that it provides a reliable map of the Hanbali school's internal landscape. For virtually every significant legal question, al-Mardawi presents: the different opinions within the school, their evidential bases, the scholars who held each view, and then — crucially — his own assessment of which opinion is the rajih (preponderant). His judgments became definitive for the school and are cited by Hanbali scholars to this day when determining the mu'tamad (operative) position.

Al-Mardawi was born in Mardah in present-day Syria and studied under the major Hanbali authorities of his age. He spent much of his career in Damascus and became a judge (qadi) there. His scholarly output beyond Al-Insaf includes Tashih al-Furu' (a companion work clarifying disputes in Ibn Muflih's Al-Furu') and Al-Tahbir Sharh al-Tahrir (on usul al-fiqh). Together these works established al-Mardawi as the definitive arbiter of Hanbali legal positions in the pre-modern period.

The significance of Al-Insaf for Islamic scholarship extends beyond the Hanbali school. It is a masterwork of comparative jurisprudence within a single school — demonstrating how a mature legal school handles its own internal diversity, resolves apparent contradictions in its transmitted positions, and maintains coherence while permitting scholarly development. Understanding this process provides important insight into how Islamic legal traditions evolve.

For scholars and advanced students of Hanbali fiqh, Al-Insaf is an indispensable reference. Works like Zad al-Mustaqni' and al-Muqni' present the ruling; Al-Insaf explains why that ruling was chosen over the alternatives within the school.
